programmes.show_description: How do they build wrecker trucks, how do they produce Roquefort, and how are trees transported? Find out as more items are examined.
How do they produce marble, how do they make mahjong pieces, and how do they build escape slides? Find out as more items are put under the microscope.
Jensen Interceptor. Mike wants a classic Jensen Interceptor. New, the car costs as much as a house, so if he does find one for his £5000, Ed might struggle to make it roadworthy.
Ford Sierra Cosworth. Mike's after a Ford Sierra Cosworth, which looks like a family vehicle and runs like a race car. But with only £5000 to spend, is his plan about to stall?
Ploughman's. Henry and Sam rummage through a tractor enthusiasts heaven, sheds and fields full of old machines. Their attention is caught by an old vintage plough.
Army Trailers And Trinkets. Henry and Sam find themselves down in the South West rummaging around another belting shed. There are so many things on offer they find it hard to choose.
Follow the production process of honey, fibre optics and bricks, and find out how each of these items is made.
Follow the production process of carpets, drinking water and acoustic guitars, and find out how each of these items is made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Matt has to use the utmost precision when restoring some stunning stained glass. Meanwhile, Jay and Dom work on an old tram seat that has been in a family for decades.
Ireland Special. Exploring Ireland's thriving antiques world, Drew and new buying partner Alister uncover treasures in private homes and showrooms across the country.
Scotland Special. Drew gets an insider's tour of Scotland with local dealer, Viki Knott. He discovers a Mackintosh-influenced chair and some sinister 1960s Americana.
The Victoria Diggers' desperately fight for survival, but they may have stumbled across a monster nugget. The Gold Gypsies fend off deadly snakes during their search.
The Gold Gypsies hunt for nuggets in ancient mine shafts and Greg discovers a rare meteorite. The Mahoneys chase for more gold using Ted's granddad's treasure map.
Tim takes up West Country skittles while Irita spins a good sailing yarn at an historic Somerset twineworks. Both buy pig themed antiques - who trots home the victor?
Angus is in Fife to dig through three generations of family clutter. They uncover a rocking horse and a collection of rare Beatrix Potter figurines.
Matt has to use the utmost precision when restoring some stunning stained glass. Meanwhile, Jay and Dom work on an old tram seat that has been in a family for decades.
Steam, Bicycles And Metal Rolling. In Cornwall, Henry and Sam find some very rare early-1900s steam-powered cars. If they're very lucky, the duo might even get to ride in one!
Signs And Wonders. In Somerset, Henry and Sam visit a man with an amazing collection of motorbikes. He has some rare finds, but are they too expensive for the guys?
Will Ralph. Henry and Fuzz visit a farm near Colchester with vintage tractors galore. The boys come away with a classic Fordson and some old traffic lights.
Robin Burford. The boys visit Rob, who has an impressive collection of vintage British bikes. They find a BSA Bantam and a classic 1930's Austin Ten automobile.
Angus visits Debbie McGee and finds a treasure trove of magical items, but what is she willing to sell? And Rav Wilding is keen to sell some family heirlooms.
Angus helps Wayne Sleep shift a wardrobe of costumes worn over his decades-spanning career. Plus, Jodie Kidd reveals a stable full of family treasures.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Tim And Camilla / Digger. Henry and Fuzz are down on the farm in Oxfordshire. They unearth mowers and tractors in Tim and Camilla's barns but end up buying something very different.
Taff / Chopper. Henry and Fuzz motor over to Taff's place in Lincoln. His extensive collection of Easy Rider-style chopper motorbikes includes an engraved 1970s Yamaha.
Last Chance Dig. The Blacklighters have just one week left to strike opal before the summer heat shuts them down. The Bushmen scramble to beat mechanical failures.
Old Mates And Motor-Racing. Richard gets into hot water as he tries to upgrade his racing licence and the Cogs head to the Isle of Man TT to help launch a new British motorbike.
A look at how products are manufactured around the world. Featuring Kamaz trucks, products for hair and the Canton Tower.
A look at how products are manufactured. Featuring ball-bearings, what goes into cartoonist's ink and discover how false eyes are made.
A look at how products are manufactured around the world. How are Russian dolls manufactured, how is aloe vera produced and how are flags made?
A look at how products are manufactured around the world. How is vodka produced, how are Chinamax ships manufactured and how is abalone made?
VW T2 Panel Van. Mike hunts out a classic camper but impossibly high prices force him to settle for a less popular panel model. Can Edd realise the ambitious plans for its upgrade?
BMW 840. It sounds like the ultimate 90s supercar bargain and Mike couldn't be more pleased with his V8, but when he sees the state of it, Edd doesn't feel as enthusiastic.
Steam, Bicycles And Metal Rolling. In Cornwall, Henry and Sam find some very rare early-1900s steam-powered cars. If they're very lucky, the duo might even get to ride in one!
Signs And Wonders. In Somerset, Henry and Sam visit a man with an amazing collection of motorbikes. He has some rare finds, but are they too expensive for the guys?
Follow the production process of tumble dryers, bubblegum and fireworks, and find out how each of these items is made.
Follow the production process of artificial eyes, dog and cat food, and mirrors, and find out how each of these items is made.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Will cleans out a table that has been damaged by insects and Brenton restores a bronze statue of a beautiful femme fatale. Dom works on a child's pedal car.
Different Is Good. Drew and Alister are on the hunt for exciting stock. They visit a business specialising in the creation of artificial flowers for film and theatre.
Treading New Ground. Treading new ground across the UK, Drew and Viki explore the factory complex of a historic British cloth producer and a sprawling reclamation yard.
The Mahoneys look for gold in one of the world's most remote regions. Meanwhile, the Victorian Diggers may have stumbled across millions of dollars' worth of gold.
The Victoria Diggers might have found a multi-million-dollar load, but they need help extracting it. The Scrappers continue to struggle with broken machines.
A very old clock ticks for Tim at Salisbury Cathedral on the last leg of his trip with Irita. She's currently out in front but will he overtake her?
Angus and the team search for interesting items at a historic home in Coventry. Later, they travel to Aberdeenshire to collect some unusual Asian furniture.
Will cleans out a table that has been damaged by insects and Brenton restores a bronze statue of a beautiful femme fatale. Dom works on a child's pedal car.
Riding Shotgun. Henry and Sam are in Suffolk, checking out a collection of vintage bikes. Henry has his eye on petrol pumps, but Sam has other ideas after seeing the sidecars.
Spoilt For Choice. In Lowestoft, Henry and Sam can't believe their luck when they check out Charlie's wares. He has everything from cars and bikes to stationary engines.
Paul Webb. Henry and Fuzz visit Paul in the Malvern Hills, a man with a perfectly maintained collection of Japanese motorbikes including a Honda 750 Four.
Frank. Henry and Fuzz are in Moretonhampstead at Frank's motoring museum - home to hundreds of cars, bikes and rare pieces of motoring memorabilia.
Anneka Rice & Antony Cotton. Angus clears the family home of TV icon Anneka Rice and looks at a collection of quirky items at soap star Antony Cotton's Lancashire cottage.
In search of treasure, Angus visits the stunning home of former royal correspondent Jennie Bond and clears soap legend Sid Owen's idyllic French retreat.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Antiques experts travel the UK with a budget of £200 each in a competition to find and buy antiques or rare items and sell them for a profit.
Chocks Away! Angus helps a widow in Lincoln find new homes for her late husband's aviation paintings. Plus, he finds a ring with some serious bling in Essex.
Musical instrument expert Pete transforms a 90-year-old child's drum kit. Meanwhile, Steve reignites an old oil lamp that belonged to Christine's grandfather.
Music expert Pete puts his skills to the test on a cherished brass instrument that's lost its lustre. Meanwhile, Dominic restores an old-school roundabout.
A look at how products are made around the world. Featuring the St Petersburg metro, the world's most expensive coffee and chip makers.
A look at how products are made. Featuring how leather is produced, how goods are moved down the Grand Canal and how Flyboard is created.
